Option B mentions "6-10% cases are associated with major congenital abnormality" - Multiple studies indicate that up to 10% of pregnancies affected by diabetes result in significant congenital anomalies.
Option C claims "1-2% of newborns are associated with single umbilical artery" - Research shows that there is indeed an increased prevalence of structural anomalies among offspring born to mothers with diabetes compared to those who do not have diabetes, supporting the likelihood of a single umbilical artery being present more frequently in diabetics' babies.
